title: Icon-only buttons lack aria-label across TopBar and ModuleSwitcher
body:
## Problem
Multiple icon-only buttons throughout the app lack visible text and accessible labels, violating WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 1.4.3 (Name, Role, Value). Screen reader users cannot identify button purposes.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/shell/TopBar.tsx:64-100` — Theme toggle (Sun/Moon icon), Focus mode (Maximize2), Always-on-top (Pin), Settings buttons render with `title` attributes but no `aria-label`
- `src/renderer/src/shell/ModuleSwitcher.tsx:26-51` — Module switcher icons (Finance, Health, Sports, etc.) use `title` but rely solely on hover text
- `src/renderer/src/shell/LaunchpadHome.tsx:280-289` — Theme and Settings buttons on launchpad render as icon-only with only `title` props
- `src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradesTable.tsx:65-70` — Delete (Trash2) button in table has no accessible label

## Expected
All icon-only buttons must have explicit `aria-label` attributes. The `title` attribute alone does not provide screen reader support; `aria-label` is required.

## Proposed fix
```tsx
// Before
<button title="Toggle theme" onClick={flipTheme}>
  {theme === 'dark' ? <Sun className="h-4 w-4" /> : <Moon className="h-4 w-4" />}
</button>

// After
<button 
  title="Toggle theme" 
  aria-label="Toggle theme (current: dark)" 
  onClick={flipTheme}
>
  {theme === 'dark' ? <Sun className="h-4 w-4" /> : <Moon className="h-4 w-4" />}
</button>
```

## Impact
Without `aria-label`, screen reader users cannot identify interactive elements, blocking access to critical controls like theme switching, settings, and module navigation. This is a barrier to full usability of the application.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3, ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) for buttons.

title: Focus-visible ring styles missing in light and dark themes
body:
## Problem
The application lacks consistent `:focus-visible` styling across interactive elements. Keyboard users relying on tab navigation cannot reliably see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css` — No `focus-visible` CSS rules defined. The file contains theme variables and launchpad styles but no focus ring definitions
- `src/renderer/src/shell/TopBar.tsx:30-100` — Buttons use basic `hover:bg-[var(--color-bg)]` but no focus-visible styles
- `src/renderer/src/shell/LaunchpadHome.tsx:513-536` — Tile buttons have `focused` state management via React props but no `:focus-visible` CSS for keyboard users
- `src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/PortfolioPage.tsx:92-100` — Tab buttons lack focus-visible ring indicators
- `src/renderer/src/modules/finance/home/SearchBar.tsx:36-86` — Search input uses `focus-within:border-[var(--color-info)]` for container but input itself has no outline or focus ring

## Expected
All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links, tabs) must have a visible 2px+ focus-visible ring that:
- Contrasts 3:1 minimum with background (WCAG AA)
- Appears in both light and dark themes
- Uses a consistent color (e.g., accent color with outline offset)

## Proposed fix
Add to `src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css`:
```css
button:focus-visible,
input:focus-visible,
select:focus-visible,
textarea:focus-visible,
[role="button"]:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--color-accent);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

## Impact
Keyboard-only users and users with motor impairments cannot navigate the UI accessibly. This blocks navigation, form submission, and control discovery.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7.

title: SearchBar dropdown lacks keyboard trap escape and ARIA live region
TradeForm inputs lack associated labels or aria-label attributes
body:
## Problem
Form inputs in TradeForm do not have associated `<label>` elements or `aria-label` attributes, making it impossible for screen reader users to identify what each field represents.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradeForm.tsx:50-90` — Six form inputs (side select, ticker, qty, price, fees, date) use only `placeholder` text for labeling
- Line 50-57: `<select>` for buy/sell has no associated label
- Lines 58-90: Multiple `<input>` fields for ticker, qty, price, fees, date, and notes lack `<label>` or `aria-label`
- Placeholder text is NOT sufficient accessibility; placeholders disappear when focused or filled

## Expected
Each form input must have one of:
1. Associated `<label>` element with matching `htmlFor` attribute
2. Explicit `aria-label` on the input
3. `aria-labelledby` pointing to an external label element

## Proposed fix
```tsx
// Before
<select value={side} onChange={(e) => setSide(e.target.value as 'buy' | 'sell')}>
  <option value="buy">Buy</option>
  <option value="sell">Sell</option>
</select>
<input
  value={ticker}
  onChange={(e) => setTicker(e.target.value)}
  placeholder="Ticker (e.g. AAPL)"
/>

// After
<label htmlFor="trade-side">Side</label>
<select id="trade-side" value={side} onChange={(e) => setSide(e.target.value as 'buy' | 'sell')}>
  <option value="buy">Buy</option>
  <option value="sell">Sell</option>
</select>
<label htmlFor="trade-ticker">Ticker</label>
<input
  id="trade-ticker"
  value={ticker}
  onChange={(e) => setTicker(e.target.value)}
  placeholder="e.g. AAPL"
  aria-label="Stock ticker symbol"
/>
```

## Impact
Screen reader users cannot fill out the trade form without external guidance. This blocks a primary feature of the portfolio management module.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Label Association), SC 3.3.2 (Labels or Instructions).

title: CommandPalette lacks Escape key announcement and focus trap management
body:
## Problem
The CommandPalette modal does not properly trap focus or announce its presence to screen readers. When opened via Cmd+K, focus may not move to the input, and no screen reader announcement confirms the modal is open.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/shell/CommandPalette.tsx:50-60` — Escape key closes palette (good) but no `aria-live` region announces modal presence
- Line 169: `if (!open) return <></>` — Modal unmounts, losing focus context
- Lines 172-235: No `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, or `aria-labelledby` on modal container
- Line 184-185: `Command.Input` has `autoFocus` but no initial announcement to screen readers
- No focus trap to prevent tab focus from escaping modal when open

## Expected
- Modal container should have `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-label="Search and run commands"`
- Initial focus should be on the input with announcement
- A focus trap should prevent tabbing outside the modal
- Escape key press should announce "Palette closed" via `aria-live`

## Proposed fix
```tsx
// Before
return (
  <div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex ..." onClick={close}>
    <div onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>
      <Command.Input autoFocus ... />

// After
return (
  <div 
    role="dialog"
    aria-modal="true"
    aria-label="Search and run commands"
    className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex ..."
    onClick={close}
  >
    <div 
      onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
      role="document"
    >
      <Command.Input 
        autoFocus 
        aria-label="Search tickers, commands, or actions"
        ...
      />
```

## Impact
Screen reader users cannot discover the command palette exists or understand its purpose. Modal behavior is unpredictable for keyboard navigators.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.3 (Focus Order), SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

title: Color contrast issues on Claude warm palette (light mode accent < 4.5:1)
body:
## Problem
The Claude warm color palette in light mode may not meet WCAG AA (4.5:1) contrast ratio for text, particularly the accent color (#cc785c) on light backgrounds. This impacts readability for users with low vision or color blindness.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css:13-26` — Light mode defines:
  ```css
  --color-fg: #1a1915;        /* ~95% brightness */
  --color-bg: #faf9f5;        /* ~98% brightness */
  --color-accent: #cc785c;    /* ~57% brightness, warm brown */
  --color-fg-muted: #6b665c;  /* ~42% brightness */
  ```
- Accent color (#cc785c) on light bg (#faf9f5) likely yields ~2.5:1 contrast, below AA threshold
- `src/renderer/src/shell/LaunchpadHome.tsx:519`: Focused tile border uses `border-[var(--color-accent)]/60` (60% opacity)
- Multiple components use accent color for text on light backgrounds without sufficient contrast

## Expected
All text colors must meet:
- **WCAG AA**: 4.5:1 ratio for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18pt+ bold or 14pt+ regular)
- Use contrast checker tools (WebAIM, Contrast Ratio) to verify light mode accent color passes

## Proposed fix
Either:
1. Darken accent color in light mode: `#b86a50` or similar darker tone
2. Use different accent for light mode, e.g., a deeper rust (#944f3d)
3. Always pair accent color with sufficient opacity/mixing to achieve 4.5:1

Example revision for `src/renderer/src/styles/globals.css`:
```css
@theme {
  --color-accent: #b86a50;  /* Darker for better contrast */
}
```

## Impact
Users with low vision, color blindness, or viewing in bright environments cannot reliably read accent-colored text. This affects design, status badges, and interactive element states.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum).

title: Prefers-reduced-motion not respected for LaunchpadHome tile animations
DetailTabs keyboard shortcut (1-11) not announced to screen readers
Tooltip-only hints on icon buttons not keyboard accessible
body:
## Problem
Many icon buttons rely on `title` attributes (which display on hover) for accessibility. Keyboard users cannot access these hints because focus does not trigger hover, and native browser focus tooltips are unreliable.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/shell/TopBar.tsx:32-100` — Multiple buttons use `title` only:
  ```tsx
  <button title="Home (Launchpad)" onClick={() => navigate('/')}>
  <button title="Launchpad (⌘H)" ...>
  <button title="Focus mode (hide shell chrome)" ...>
  <button title="Always on top" ...>
  <button title="Toggle theme" ...>
  <button title="Settings" ...>
  ```
- `src/renderer/src/shell/ModuleSwitcher.tsx:17-51` — Module nav buttons use `title` but not `aria-label`
- `src/renderer/src/shell/PageHeader.tsx:36` — Back button title: `title={back ? 'Back' : 'Launchpad (⌘H)'}`

## Expected
- Favor `aria-label` over `title` for button identification
- If using `title`, pair with explicit `aria-label` or visible label on focus
- Consider adding visible text labels for critical buttons (Settings, Theme Toggle)
- Use CSS `focus:ring` and pseudo-elements for keyboard-visible focus hints

## Proposed fix
```tsx
// Before
<button
  onClick={togglePalette}
  title="Focus mode (hide shell chrome)"
  className="rounded-md p-1.5 ..."
>
  <Maximize2 className="h-4 w-4" />
</button>

// After
<button
  onClick={togglePalette}
  aria-label="Toggle focus mode (hides shell chrome)"
  className="rounded-md p-1.5 ..."
>
  <Maximize2 className="h-4 w-4" />
</button>
```

## Impact
Keyboard users and screen reader users cannot discover button purposes without hovering. This reduces accessibility of critical controls.

## Related
WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.13 (Content on Hover or Focus).

title: TradesTable missing table semantics: scope, caption, and ARIA attributes
body:
## Problem The TradesTable uses basic `` structure but lacks semantic enhancements for screen readers. Column headers lack `scope` attributes, no table caption explains purpose, and delete button is icon-only. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/finance/portfolio/TradesTable.tsx:19-31` — Table structure: ```tsx
{/* ... more headers */} {/* rows */}
Date Ticker
``` - `` elements have no `scope="col"` attribute - No `` element describing the table (e.g., "Trade Log") - Delete button (Line 65-70) has no `aria-label`; just an icon ## Expected - Each header `` must have `scope="col"` - Empty headers should use `aria-hidden="true"` or be labeled - Add `` before ``: "Trade Log — Records of all buy and sell transactions" - Delete button should have `aria-label="Delete trade {tradeId}"` ## Proposed fix ```tsx // Before ... // After
Date Ticker
... {trades.map((t) => ( {/* ... */} ))}
Trade Log — All recorded buy and sell transactions
Date Ticker
``` ## Impact Screen reader users cannot understand table structure or purpose. Navigation through data rows is confusing without proper header association. ## Related WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), H51 (Using table markup).
